history.qwen_observation: Springfox dependency and its SwaggerConfig class are incompatible with Spring Boot 3, causing a compilation error for a missing getName symbol after the initial upgrade attempt.
history.qwen_rationale: Explicitly removing Springfox dependencies before re-running the Spring Boot 3 upgrade recipe allows the meta-recipe to cleanly transition the project to Jakarta EE and SpringDoc, which typically deletes or replaces the broken SwaggerConfig class automatically.

history.qwen_observation: The SpringFoxToSpringDoc recipe transformed SwaggerConfig.java to use SpringDoc classes (GroupedOpenApi) but failed to add the corresponding SpringDoc dependency to the POM, causing a compilation error.
history.qwen_rationale: Pre-pending a POM_PATCH step to explicitly add the springdoc-openapi-ui dependency ensures the required classes are on the classpath when the migration recipe runs, resolving the missing package error and allowing the chain to proceed to the Spring Boot 3 upgrade.

history.qwen_rationale: Switching to springdoc-openapi-ui 1.8.0 ensures the GroupedOpenApi class is available on the compile classpath. The chain then proceeds with the SpringFox-to-SpringDoc migration, followed by the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade, and finally the Java 21 plugin/build upgrades and transforms.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed in SwaggerConfig.java because org.springdoc.core.models.GroupedOpenApi was not found on the classpath, indicating the springdoc-openapi-ui dependency was not correctly resolved or added before the SpringFoxToSpringDoc recipe ran.
history.qwen_rationale: Switching to org.openrewrite.maven.AddDependency ensures the springdoc-openapi-ui dependency is properly injected into the POM before the migration recipe executes. Using version 1.8.0 provides the required GroupedOpenApi class, allowing the SpringFoxToSpringDoc recipe to successfully rewrite SwaggerConfig.java and compile under JDK 17 before proceeding to the Spring Boot 3 and JDK 21 upgrades.
